original_text: Amazon finished the fourth quarter of 2025, with $177 billion in revenue.
text: 亚马逊 2025 年第四季度营收为 1770 亿美元。

original_text: AMD reported a record-breaking $34.6 billion for the full year.
text: AMD 公布全年营收达到创纪录的 346 亿美元。

original_text: If you look strictly at the headlines, these tech giants appear to be generating wealth across every level of their business.
text: 如果你严格审视头条新闻，这些科技巨头似乎在其各个业务层面都创造了财富。

original_text: Take IBM.
text: 以IBM为例。

original_text: Under CEO, Arvind Krishna, the company pulled in $67.5 billion over the year.
text: 在首席执行官 Arvind Krishna 的领导下，该公司全年收入达 675 亿美元。

original_text: Krishna closed 2025 by highlighting strong, broad-based performance and expressing confidence in their momentum for 2026.
text: Krishna 在 2025 年结束时强调了强劲、广泛的业绩，并对 2026 年的势头表示信心。

original_text: From a high-level executive view, corporate health looks robust, but those top-line numbers act as a mask.
text: 从高层管理人员的角度来看，企业健康状况看起来很强劲，但这些顶线数字只是一个面具。

original_text: By aggregating billions of dollars into a single sum, corporations can obscure deep structural vulnerabilities within their specific departments.
text: 通过将数十亿美元汇总成一笔，企业可以掩盖其特定部门内深层的结构性漏洞。

original_text: A massive total revenue figure is no longer a reliable indicator of a company's true trajectory or its internal stability.
text: 庞大的总收入数字不再是衡量公司真实发展轨迹或其内部稳定性的可靠指标。

original_text: To find where the money is actually going, we have to look at segment reporting.
text: 为了找到资金的实际去向，我们必须查看分部报告。

original_text: This is a granular accounting practice that breaks down exactly which specific business units or regions generated the cash.
text: 这是一种精细的会计实践，可以准确细分哪些特定业务部门或地区产生了现金。

original_text: Stripping away that total revenue reveals a discordant web of internal units.
text: 除去总收入，就会发现内部部门的网络不和谐。

original_text: Inside a single company, different departments often operate at vastly different speeds.
text: 在一家公司内部，不同部门的运作速度往往截然不同。

original_text: One might be scaling rapidly, while another is in a state of active decline.
text: 一个可能正在迅速扩张，而另一个则处于积极衰退的状态。

original_text: To understand the 2025 economy, you have to look past the brand name and analyze the individual segments operating within it.
text: 要了解 2025 年的经济，您必须超越品牌名称并分析其中运营的各个细分市场。

original_text: The first major trend in the Q4 data is the surge in infrastructure.
text: 第四季度数据的第一个主要趋势是基础设施的激增。

original_text: The foundational physical layer of the tech economy is currently seeing an explosion in demand.
text: 科技经济的基础物理层目前的需求正在爆炸式增长。

original_text: This bar chart shows the divide inside AMD.
text: 该条形图显示了 AMD 内部的分歧。

original_text: Their data center segment revenue surged 39% to $5.38 billion.
text: 他们的数据中心部门收入猛增 39%，达到 53.8 亿美元。

original_text: A growth rate that makes their other revenue streams look stagnant by comparison.
text: 相比之下，这种增长率使得他们的其他收入来源显得停滞不前。

original_text: Adding Broadcom to the chart shows a nearly identical pattern.
text: 将博通添加到图表中显示出几乎相同的模式。

original_text: Their semiconductor solution segment saw a 35% jump, confirming a sector-wide boom in the hardware that powers high-end computing.
text: 他们的半导体解决方案领域增长了 35%，证实了支持高端计算的硬件在整个行业的繁荣。

original_text: This allocation view shows the material manufacturer coherent.
text: 此分配视图显示了材料制造商的一致性。

original_text: Their data center and communications unit now accounts for 72% of their total revenue, dominating the rest of their business.
text: 他们的数据中心和通信部门目前占其总收入的 72%，主导着其余业务。

original_text: The physical consequences are visible in companies like Vertiv, which builds the thermal management systems for these facilities.
text: 在像 Vertiv 这样为这些设施构建热管理系统的公司中，物理后果是显而易见的。

original_text: Their America segment grew 50% to meet the demand for cooling and powering this hardware.
text: 他们的美国业务增长了 50%，以满足该硬件的冷却和供电需求。

original_text: This intense, localized growth acts up camouflage.
text: 这种强烈的、局部的生长起到了伪装的作用。

original_text: The volume of cash flowing into these specific infrastructure units effectively hides the lack of progress in other departments.
text: 流入这些特定基础设施部门的现金流量有效地掩盖了其他部门缺乏进展的情况。

original_text: The infrastructure sector has become a primary life support system for these massive corporations.
text: 基础设施部门已成为这些大型企业的主要生命支持系统。

original_text: Moving up the tech stack to the software layer, the data reveals a high-velocity commerce engine driving the consumer side of the business.
text: 将技术堆栈提升到软件层，数据揭示了驱动消费者方面业务的高速商业引擎。

original_text: Amazon's results illustrate the split.
text: 亚马逊的业绩说明了这种分歧。

original_text: Their AWS segment, their massive cloud computing platform jumped 24%.
text: 他们的 AWS 业务，即庞大的云计算平台，增长了 24%。

original_text: That cloud growth is mirrored by their advertising revenue, which spiked 22%, and added $12 billion in new revenue over the course of the year.
text: 他们的广告收入也反映了云计算的增长，广告收入猛增 22%，并在这一年中增加了 120 亿美元的新收入。

original_text: This advertising growth extends to Reddit, where US revenue jumped 68%, and revenue from small and medium-sized businesses doubled.
text: 这种广告增长趋势延伸至 Reddit，其美国收入增长了 68%，来自中小企业的收入翻了一番。

original_text: Digital targeting is also impacting physical retail.
text: 数字定位也正在影响实体零售。

original_text: Walmart's e-commerce segment grew 28%, marking its seventh straight quarter of growth above 20%.
text: 沃尔玛的电子商务部门增长了 28%，这是连续第七个季度增长超过 20%。

original_text: Globally, the South Korean retailer, Kupong, saw its developing offering segment grow 32%, fueled by rapid customer engagement in the Taiwanese market.
text: 在全球范围内，韩国零售商 Kupong 的发展中产品部门增长了 32%，这得益于台湾市场客户的快速参与。

original_text: The consumer economy has concentrated into these specific highly algorithmic commerce segments.
text: 消费经济已集中到这些特定的高度算法化的商业领域。

original_text: But for every high growth engine, the earnings data reveals segments that are being left behind.
text: 但对于每一个高增长引擎，盈利数据都揭示了被抛在后面的细分市场。

original_text: This chart maps the conflict within TripAdvisor.
text: 该图表反映了 TripAdvisor 内部的冲突。

original_text: Their modern experiences segment is trending upward with 18% growth, while their legacy hotel segment plunges with declines as high as 23%.
text: 他们的现代体验细分市场呈上升趋势，增长了 18%，而他们的传统酒店细分市场则大幅下滑，降幅高达 23%。

original_text: Adding coherence industrial segment shows a similar trend.
text: 增加一致性工业领域也显示出类似的趋势。

original_text: It recorded 0% growth, further evidence of stagnation in legacy manufacturing.
text: 增长率为 0%，进一步证明传统制造业陷入停滞。

original_text: Management now treats these stagnant legacy divisions as financial weights that actively drag down the enterprise's overall performance.
text: 管理层现在将这些停滞不前的遗留部门视为积极拖累企业整体业绩的财务负担。

original_text: Now, when we synthesize this map of the 2025 segment data, we see the clear operational strategy driving these companies.
text: 现在，当我们综合这张 2025 年细分数据图时，我们看到了推动这些公司的明确运营战略。

original_text: Corporate boards are now focused on capital reallocation, the shifting of resources from one part of the company to another.
text: 公司董事会现在的重点是资本重新分配，即将资源从公司的一个部门转移到另一个部门。

original_text: Executives are siphoning budgets away from their legacy segments to fuel the growth of their high-performing tech units.
text: 高管们正在从传统部门抽走预算，以推动高性能科技部门的增长。

original_text: The data shows that maintaining an enterprise now requires a zero sum trade-off, where underperforming segments are cannibalized to keep high growth engines running.
text: 数据显示，现在维持一个企业需要零和权衡，表现不佳的部门被蚕食以保持高增长引擎的运行。

original_text: The traditional monolithic corporation has fractured into a collection of disjointed business units.
text: 传统的单一公司已经分裂成一系列互不相干的业务部门。

original_text: A company's brand identity no longer reflects its financial reality.
text: 公司的品牌形象不再反映其财务现实。

original_text: A famous nameplate tells you very little about where the actual profit is being generated.
text: 著名的铭牌几乎无法告诉您实际利润是在哪里产生的。

original_text: Moving forward, observers must look past the ticker symbol and focus on the specific internal unit that is actually driving the growth.
text: 展望未来，观察者必须超越股票代码，关注真正推动增长的特定内部部门。

original_text: In the modern economy, you aren't investing in the company. You are investing in the segment.
text: 在现代经济中，你并不是在投资公司。您正在投资该细分市场。
